input.background: The four patients illustrative example has established a binomial random variable in which n=4 and p=0.75.
input.question: Calculate the mean and variance of the binomial distribution.

output.answer: Therefore, this distribution has μ=n p=4 ⋅ 0.75=3 and σ²=n p q=4 ⋅ 0.75 ⋅(1-0.75)=0.75.

n.description: The number of trials in the binomial distribution.

p.description: The probability of success in each trial of the binomial distribution.

mean.description: The mean of the binomial distribution, calculated as the product of the number of trials and the probability of success.

variance.description: The variance of the binomial distribution, calculated as the product of the number of trials, the probability of success, and the probability of failure.

meta_info.caption: Expected value and variance of a binomial random vari-  able (Four patients example).
meta_info.section: 6.5 Expected Value and Variance of a  Binomial Random Variable
